data.title: Swiss Bank UBS (UBS) Forecasts Global Stock Market Rally in 2026
data.description:
Despite some geopolitical headwinds, Swiss bank UBS (UBS) expects a global rally in stocks this year.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts at the bank see stocks around the world rising as performance widens beyond mega-cap technology companies such as Alphabet (GOOGL) and Nvidia (NVDA) and into more cyclical parts of the market.

In a new report, UBS writes that, "global equities continue to grind higher, albeit at a slower pace," but stressed that "performance continued to broaden, with cyclical regions and sectors sharply outperforming recently." UBS says stocks in the U.S., Asia and Europe should benefit in coming months from a powerful mix of catalysts.

AI Still a Tailwind for Stocks

UBS still sees artificial intelligence (AI) as an "important tailwind" for the global market, especially as the focus among players in the space shifts to monetization. However, global markets should also benefit from easing tariff risks, further interest rate cuts, and supportive fiscal policies around the world.

UBS adds that the "rare combination of supportive monetary and fiscal policies" should underpin corporate earnings in 2026, with the bank forecasting "close to 12% EPS growth for MSCI AC World" stocks and expecting performance to broaden throughout the year ahead.

Is UBS Stock a Buy?

UBS stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 13 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on eight Buy, two Hold, and three Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average UBS price target of $46 implies 2.95% downside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Novo Nordisk Stock (NVO) Gains on Obesity Pill Demand as Goldman Flags Earnings Upside
data.description:
Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk's (NVO) shares rose about 3% as data showed that over 18,000 prescriptions were issued in the first week of the launch of the Wegovy obesity pill. The stock appreciation also follows a new Goldman Sachs (GS) Buy rating citing near-term earnings gains.

data.title: Amazon (AMZN) to Cut 14,000 Jobs Ahead of Earnings on Feb. 5
data.description:
E-commerce giant Amazon (AMZN) is widely expected to cut an additional 14,000 jobs ahead of its next financial results being issued on Feb. 5.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The job cuts are expected to target Amazon's corporate workers who were subject to a similar round of layoffs last autumn. At that time, management at Amazon said they were planning a total of 30,000 job cuts to the company's corporate staff.

The latest workforce reduction continues a trend at Amazon, which eliminated 27,000 jobs in 2022 and 2023. The latest cuts are aimed at reducing layers of management and would represent about 10% of Amazon's corporate staff, with the rest of its 1.58 million employees working in warehouses.

Amazon's Automation Push

The cuts, which could begin as soon as Tuesday, are expected to affect Amazon Web Services, retail, Prime Video, and human resources.

White-collar jobs
In October, Amazon employees were told they would remain on the payroll for 90 days, and could apply for jobs internally or seek other employment during that time, with the period ending on Monday.

In 2017, Amazon employed only a fraction of its current workforce, with about 541,000 people following the acquisition of Whole Foods, and was already the second-largest US-based company by headcount, after Walmart.

Is AMZN Stock a Buy?

AMZN stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 47 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 46 Buy and one Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average AMZN price target of $294.93 implies 27.34% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Novo Nordisk's Wegovy Pill Records Nearly 20,000 Prescriptions in First Week
data.description:
Novo Nordisk's (NVO) new weight-loss pill recorded 18,410 prescriptions in the U.S. during its first full week of availability, according to market data provided by IQVIA (IQV).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts and investors are closely watching sales and prescription data for Novo Nordisk's Wegovy pill to see if the Danish pharmaceutical company can gain market share and leverage its first-mover advantage over archrival Eli Lilly (LLY) in an increasingly competitive weight-loss market.

Novo Nordisk beat Eli Lilly to market with its weight-loss pill and the European pharma giant is counting on the medication to be a blockbuster and help it regain market share after issuing profit warnings and seeing its growth decline over the past year.

Popular with Consumers

Weight-loss pills are expected to be a gamechanger for both Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly. Until now, weight-loss medications were taken by injection using needles. Pills are anticipated to be more palatable and popular with consumers, leading to increased sales.

In a note to clients, Guggenheim wrote that the number of Novo Nordisk's weight-loss pill prescriptions out of the gate "is tracking ahead of other GLP-1 launches." NVO stock has risen about 25% so far this year, largely due to high expectations for the Wegovy pill.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Gold Set for Best Week Since 2020 on Elevated Safe-Haven Demand
data.description:
Last year, gold (XAUUSD) posted its best performance since 1979. The momentum has only continued into 2026, as the precious metal is set to gain more than 7% this week, marking its best weekly gain since March 2020.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gold tends to rise during periods of geopolitical uncertainty and received a boost after President Trump threatened tariffs on eight European countries until the U.S. reaches a deal for Greenland. Trump later backed off on the threat while adding that he would soon impose a 25% secondary tariff on any country doing business with Iran.

Goldman Lifts December Gold Forecast to $5,400

Gold surpassed $4,900 per troy ounce earlier this week and is currently hovering below $5,000. By the end of the year, it could reach $5,400, according to a new estimate from Goldman Sachs (GS). The bank cited demand from the private sector driven by "global policy uncertainty."

Goldman added that most private sector purchases of gold will come through exchange-traded funds (ETFs). Lower interest rates could also benefit gold because it doesn't pay out interest. In other words, the opportunity cost of holding gold compared to interest-bearing assets goes down with lower rates.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: AMD Stock Jumps on Ryzen 7 9850X3D Price & Release Date Reveal
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock was on the rise Friday after the price of the semiconductor company's newest CPU was revealed. AMD has set the MSRP for its Ryzen 7 9850X3D at $499, which is roughly what consumers and investors expected based on other CPU prices and leaks.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AMD's Ryzen 7 9850X3D is the company's new flagship CPU for gamers, surpassing the performance of the Ryzen 7 9800X3D. The price of the Ryzen 7 9850X3D reflects this, as it is priced slightly above the Ryzen 7 9800X3D's MSRP of $479.

David McAfee, vice president and general manager of AMD's client-side business, posted about the new CPU on X, formerly Twitter. He said, "The world's most advanced gaming processor just got faster. Built for gamers who demand smooth, relentless performance when it matters most." McAfee also confirmed the $499 MSRP and revealed a Jan. 29, 2026 release date for the CPU.

AMD Stock Movement Today

AMD stock was up 2.62% on Friday, extending an 18.48% rally year-to-date. The stock has also increased 106.55% over the past 12 months.

AMD stock movement today was strong, and with it came heavy trading. As of this writing, more than 24 million shares have changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average of about 29 million units.
data.author: William White

data.title: 'Here's the Reason,' Says Top Investor About Netflix Stock
data.description:
Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) did it again, delivering another strong year of growth.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"In 2025, we met or exceeded all of our financial objectives," began the company's Q4 shareholder letter. And indeed, a look at the numbers shows a large company getting bigger.

Full-year revenues grew to $45.2 billion, an increase of 16% year-over-year (and 17% on a currency-neutral basis). Its 2025 operating margin increased by 3% to 29.5%, the company crossed 325 million paid subscribers, and its branded original content enjoyed a 9% rise in viewership.

The market didn't rush to reward Netflix, though. Quite the opposite occurred, and NFLX's share price has continued on its downward trajectory. Part of the reason seems to be ongoing worries about the massive amount of cash — $72 billion — the company is ready to part with to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery.

There's another reason top investor Daniel Sparks is proceeding cautiously.

"One factor in particular looks like an especially good reason for investors to be concerned: management's guidance for significantly slower constant-currency revenue growth in 2026 compared to 2025," explains the 5-star investor.

That's not to say Sparks is disappointed in the company's Q4 performance. He calls the last quarter "exceptional," pointing to accelerating revenue growth of 17.6% year-over-year (up from 17.2% in Q3), increasing operating margin of 24.5% (up from 22.2% in Q4 2024), and overall growth in advertising revenue ($1.5 billion for 2025, more than two and a half times its 2024 amount), among other successes.

And yet, Sparks is fixated on the company's more "subdued outlook" when it comes to its revenue forecast. He notes that its expected revenue growth in constant currency for 2026 of 11% to 13% doesn't compare favorably to the 14% to 17% guidance it gave one year ago.

Given that the company trades at a "premium" price-to-earnings valuation in the mid-30s, Sparks acknowledges that it makes sense for shares to drop with growth appearing to slow.

At the end of the day, that's the reason that Sparks won't be tuning into NFLX at present.

"I don't think this is the right time to buy shares in the streaming service company's stock," sums up Sparks. "For now, I think the best move is to be patient." (To watch Daniel Sparks' track record, click here)

Wall Street seems content to "Netflix and Chill" for now, however. With 26 Buys, 10 Holds, and 1 Sell, NFLX enjoys a Moderate Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $116.42 implies gains just shy of 40%. (See NFLX stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: U.S.-EU Weigh $800 Billion Fund to Rebuild Ukraine
data.description:
The U.S. and the European Union are considering opening an $800 billion fund to help rebuild Ukraine once the war against Russia ends, according to an 18-page document shared with EU leaders and reviewed by Politico. The document also includes an expedited plan for Ukraine to join the EU.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

If enacted, the plan would go into effect once Ukraine and Russia reach a ceasefire. However, a ceasefire remains uncertain as fighting continues and negotiations between the two countries have yet to deliver a breakthrough.

Ukraine-Russia War Creates Major Investment Barrier

BlackRock (BLK) Vice Chairman Philipp Hildebrand noted that attracting funding to rebuild Ukraine will be difficult until the war comes to an end. "Think about it. If you're a pension fund, you're fiduciary towards your clients, your pensioners. It's nearly impossible to invest into a war zone," he said at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland.

Representatives from the U.S., Ukraine, and Russia are set to meet in Abu Dhabi on Friday and Saturday as the war approaches its fourth anniversary.
